wallet-tx-lifecycle.title: Wallet UX & Transaction lifecycle
wallet-tx-lifecycle.description: Simulate what happens when you click “confirm” in a wallet: nonces, EIP-1559 fees, mempool inclusion, replacement (speed up / cancel), and common revert reasons.

keyTakeaways.0: Your nonce enforces per-account ordering; a gap blocks later txs from being mined
keyTakeaways.1: With EIP-1559, you set max fee + priority fee; effective gas price is min(maxFee, baseFee + priorityFee)
keyTakeaways.2: Wallets check affordability using the cap: value + gasLimit × maxFee (not today’s effective price)
keyTakeaways.3: If base fee rises above max fee, the tx is ignored until base fee drops (or you replace it)
keyTakeaways.4: Speed up / cancel replaces the pending tx (same nonce) and typically requires a fee bump (≈10%)
